data.title: Crane shares dip after hours as sluggish orders temper Q4 results
data.description:
Crane (CR) shares fell about 1% in extended trading Monday after the company reported fourth-quarter results that exceeded Wall Street expectations but flagged softer demand in part of its portfolio and a 2026 outlook that brackets, but does not clearly top, consensus forecasts.

The Stamford, Conn.-based manufacturer posted adjusted earnings of $1.53 a share from continuing operations for the fourth quarter, ahead of the Wall Street consensus estimate of $1.41 a share. Revenue rose to $581 million, above analyst expectations of $572 million.

Net income rose to about $82 million, or $1.39 a share, from roughly $71 million, or $1.20 a share, a year earlier.

Fourth-quarter sales increased 6.8%, driven by 5.4% core growth, modest acquisition contributions and favorable foreign exchange. Operating profit climbed 18.1% to $101.8 million, reflecting higher productivity and pricing.

M&A margin pressure

Investors appeared to focus on several cautionary signals in the report. Core orders grew just 2.4% in the quarter, while management said orders in the process flow technologies segment remained sluggish. That unit posted a 1.5% core sales decline and saw backlog fall to $359.9 million at year-end from $383.0 million three months earlier.

Crane (CR) also indicated that recent acquisitions will pressure margins in the near term. The company said its 2026 adjusted segment operating margin is expected to be about 22.5% or higher, compared with a comparable 23.7% in 2025, citing initial dilution from acquisitions. In addition, higher interest expense tied to acquisition financing is expected to lift net non-operating expense to about $58 million next year.

2026 guidance

For 2026, Crane (CR) initiated adjusted earnings guidance of $6.55 to $6.75 a share, reflecting about 10% growth at the midpoint. The outlook compares with the current Wall Street estimate of $6.67 a share and implies limited upside without stronger order momentum or faster synergy realization.

Chief Executive Officer Max Mitchell said in a statement that the quarter capped “another outstanding year,” highlighting record sales and profit in the company’s growth segments.

Crane (CR) ended the year with $506.5 million in cash and $1.15 billion in debt following the closing of multiple acquisitions. Management said the acquisition contribution in 2026 will be largely offset by interest expense, with more meaningful earnings benefits expected in 2027 and beyond.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (ABT) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock closed 1.2% higher at $108.77 on Monday.

The MedTech giant started its downward trend on Jan. 15. The stock has lost over 13% in the preceding six sessions.

ABT closed 1% lower on Friday at $107.42. January has been a less favorable month for the company, as it traded 12 sessions in red and just three sessions in green.

Last week, the Illinois-based company’s shares closed 10% lower on Thursday after it reported lower-than-expected revenue for Q4 2025 following underperformance in its Nutrition segment.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, ABT has a Hold rating with a score of 3.06 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got D- in the growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 21 analysts gave ABT a Buy and above, eight analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower. Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

“The recent pullback in Abbott Laboratories appears to be driven more by short-term sentiment than by any deterioration in its long-term fundamentals. Near-term weakness in the Nutrition and Diagnostics segments has pressured results. However, ABT continues to deliver solid earnings growth, expanding margins, and strong performance in high-growth areas,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis by Gen Alpha.

Another Seeking Alpha analysts also argued that a post-earnings sell-off and a price dip are buying opportunities for investors.

Earlier in the day, Abbott said its Chairman and CEO Robert Ford purchased 18.8K of the company’s stock recently with a value of just over $2M.

The stock rose 10% last year, compared to the 16% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

data.title: Customers Bank appoints Stephen Wyremski as COO
data.description:
• Customers Bank, a subsidiary of Customers Bancorp (CUBI) on Monday announced the appointment of Steve Wyremski as Chief Operating Officer, effective January 26, 2026.
  • Wyremski joined Customers Bank in 2024 as a strategic advisor. Prior to joining Customers Bank, Wyremski served as CFO of a New York-based commercial bank with over $100 billion in assets. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Crane Non-GAAP EPS of $1.53 beats by $0.12, revenue of $581M beats by $9.01M
data.description:
• Crane press release (CR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.53 beats by $0.12.
  • Revenue of $581M (+6.8% Y/Y) beats by $9.01M.
  • Initiating full year 2026 adjusted EPS outlook of $6.55-$6.75 reflecting 10% growth versus 2025 at the midpoint on a comparable basis that now excludes after-tax, acquisition-related intangible amortization in both years, and excluding hurricane-related insurance recoveries in 2025.
